name: The Paseo Maritimo of Valencia
region_intro: Hiking around Valence offers access to diverse landscapes, situated between the hills of Ardèche and the Vercors mountains. The region features a mix of forests, rolling hills, and river valleys, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Natural features include the Monts du Matin with its cliffs and panoramas, as well as gorges and rural countryside. The Drôme region, where Valence is located, is characterized by green hills and peaceful rivers. Best hiking trails around Valence The most popular hiking route is Turia Gardens and Valencia Historic Center Loop, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This easy route explores urban green spaces and historic areas. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Paseo Maritimo of Valencia, an easy 4.8 miles (7.7 km) path. This trail follows the coastline, offering views of the sea. Local hikers also love the Gola de Pujol and Pujol Lake Loop, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail leading through natural parkland, often completed in about 1 hour 16 minutes. Hiking around Valence is defined by diverse environments including forests, hills, and river paths. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks. The routes in Valence are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 800 reviews. More than 10,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Valence's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Valence?
A: There are over 390 hiking trails around Valence, offering a wide variety of experiences. This includes 246 easy routes, 138 moderate trails, and 11 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: Are there easy hikes with scenic views near Valence?
A: Yes, many easy hikes around Valence offer beautiful views. The region is known for its rolling hills and proximity to the Vercors mountains, providing panoramic vistas. For example, the Monts du Matin, at the foot of the Vercors, feature paths with breathtaking panoramas. Even within Valence, parks like Jouvet Park offer views of the Vercors.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking near Valence?
A: Hiking around Valence offers incredibly diverse landscapes. You can explore the foothills of the majestic Vercors Mountains, discover forests and tranquil hills, or follow paths along peaceful rivers in the Drôme region. Natural features like the Gorges de la Combe d'Oyans and rural countryside with views of the Vercors plateau are also common.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Valence?
A: Yes, circular routes are popular in the Valence area. Many trails are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example of a loop trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e828428605/gola-de-pujol-and-pujol-lake-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gola de Pujol and Pujol Lake Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through natural parkland.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Valence?
A: The hiking trails around Valence are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 870 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse environments, from urban green spaces to mountain foothills, and the variety of options suitable for all abilities.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options near Valence?
A: Valence offers many family-friendly hiking options. The region's diverse environments, including forests, hills, and urban parks, provide opportunities for all ages. Easy, shorter routes are plentiful, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e827099215/turia-gardens-and-valencia-historic-center-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turia Gardens and Valencia Historic Center Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which explores urban green spaces and historic areas, perfect for a leisurely family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Valence?
A: Many trails around Valence are suitable for dogs, though it's always recommended to check specific trail regulations or local signage. Generally, the natural parks and rural paths are welcoming to well-behaved dogs on a leash, allowing you and your pet to enjoy the diverse landscapes together.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes around Valence?
A: Hikes around Valence vary greatly in duration. Many easy routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e827267629/the-paseo-maritimo-of-valencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo Maritimo of Valencia&lt;/a&gt;, can be completed in under 2 hours. Longer, more challenging treks in the Vercors foothills might take several hours. With over 390 routes, you can find options ranging from short strolls to full-day adventures.

Q: Are there any trails that follow rivers or coastlines near Valence?
A: Yes, the region offers trails that follow waterways. The Drôme region is characterized by peaceful rivers, providing scenic paths. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e827267629/the-paseo-maritimo-of-valencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo Maritimo of Valencia&lt;/a&gt; follows the coastline, offering beautiful views of the sea, while the extensive Viarhôna cycle route also accommodates pedestrians along the Rhône.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for on hikes?
A: When hiking near Valence, you can look forward to several notable natural features. The Vercors Mountains provide magnificent backdrops and challenging trails. The Monts du Matin offer striking cliffs and rolling hills. You might also encounter natural gorges like the Gorges de la Combe d'Oyans, and various forests and lakes throughout the Valence Romans area.

Q: Is public transport accessible to hiking trailheads near Valence?
A: Valence has a public transport network that can provide access to some trailheads, especially those closer to urban or suburban areas. For trails further afield, particularly in the Vercors foothills or more remote natural areas, personal transport might be more convenient. It's advisable to check local bus routes for specific trail access points.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers around Valence?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers, Valence offers challenging hikes, particularly in the nearby Vercors Mountains. The GR 93 long-distance hiking path, for instance, originates in the Rhône Valley and traverses the Vercors, leading to demanding terrain and significant elevation changes. There are 11 routes classified as 'difficult' in the area, providing a good test for seasoned hikers.
tips: It is the beach of Valencia's old fishing district, from which it takes its name. The Valencians still call it Las Arenas, after the ancient spa town of the same name. With a width of almost 200 meters, everyone will find a place.
